_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use short phrases to ask for things, say yes to things, or say no to things.

_source.w: We are learning to use short phrases to ask for things, say yes to things, or say no to things.
y: Using phrases helps people understand exactly what we want or need.
When we can ask and respond with phrases, we join in more conversations.
This builds our confidence in communicating with the people around us.
eg: We point to a picture of a tree in a book and say 'big tree' to describe what we see.
We say 'more please' when we want another turn at an activity.
We use our device to say 'no thanks' when offered something we do not want.
We combine two words like 'want ball' to request something specific.
We say 'yes' and nod when asked if we would like to join an activity.
We use a short phrase like 'my turn' during a game.
